name: Freiburg
blurb: Freiburg is a sun-drenched, eco-conscious city on the edge of the Black Forest, known for its charming historic old town, winding Bächle streams, and vibrant student life.
imageAlt: The Freiburg Minster towering over the historic market square with colorful buildings and the Bächle (small water channels) in the foreground.

name: Greifswald
blurb: Greifswald is a charming Hanseatic city in Germany, blending medieval brick Gothic architecture with a lively student atmosphere. Expect historic market squares, a picturesque museum harbour, and the inspiring landscapes of Caspar David Friedrich.
imageAlt: Historic market square in Greifswald with the Rathaus and colorful gabled houses under a blue sky.

name: Nuremberg
blurb: Nuremberg, Bavaria's second-largest city, is a captivating blend of medieval charm and profound modern history. Expect gingerbread, bratwurst, and a deep dive into centuries of German narrative, from imperial glory to the solemn past of the Nazi era.
imageAlt: Nuremberg Imperial Castle overlooking the medieval Old Town with half-timbered houses and a river

name: Rothenburg ob der Tauber
blurb: Step into a real-life fairytale in Rothenburg ob der Tauber, Germany's best-preserved medieval walled town, where every cobblestone street whispers tales of centuries past.
imageAlt: Rothenburg ob der Tauber - a view of the iconic Plönlein with its half-timbered houses and two towers

name: Rostock
blurb: Rostock, a Hanseatic city on Germany's Baltic coast, blends medieval brick Gothic architecture with a lively student scene and the laid-back beach vibe of nearby Warnemünde. Expect fresh seafood, historic streets, and a refreshing sea breeze.
imageAlt: View of Rostock's Neuer Markt with the historic Town Hall and St. Marienkirche in the background

name: Cologne
blurb: Forget the cliches; Cologne is a city that blends ancient Roman history with a vibrant, modern edge. Come for the towering cathedral, stay for the Kölsch, the art, and the surprisingly lively nightlife.
imageAlt: Cologne Cathedral and Hohenzollern Bridge at sunset, with love locks visible on the bridge and the Rhine River flowing below.

name: Bad Doberan & Molli Train
blurb: Experience the charm of a bygone era with a ride on the historic Molli steam train, connecting Bad Doberan's grand Minster to the elegant seaside resorts of Heiligendamm and Kühlungsborn.
imageAlt: The historic Molli steam train passing through a tree-lined avenue in Bad Doberan.

name: Baden-Baden
blurb: This elegant spa town in Germany's Black Forest offers a blend of Roman bath traditions, Belle Époque luxury, and stunning natural beauty. Come for the thermal waters, stay for the refined charm.
imageAlt: A panoramic view of Baden-Baden, showing the elegant Belle Époque architecture, lush trees, and the rolling hills of the Black Forest in the background.

name: Dresden
blurb: Dresden, the 'Florence on the Elbe,' is a city reborn from wartime ashes, blending Baroque grandeur with a vibrant modern edge. Discover its stunning architecture, world-class museums, and bohemian quarters.
imageAlt: View of the Frauenkirche and Neumarkt square in Dresden at dusk, with lights reflecting on the cobblestones.

name: Fischland-Darß-Zingst
blurb: Escape the city for the wild, windswept beauty of Fischland-Darß-Zingst, a Baltic Sea peninsula famed for its pristine beaches, artistic villages, and dramatic natural landscapes.
imageAlt: A view of the Weststrand (Western Beach) on Fischland-Darß-Zingst with gnarled trees and Baltic Sea waves.

name: Rügen Island
blurb: Germany's largest island, Rügen boasts dramatic chalk cliffs, sandy beaches, and charming seaside resorts, making for a refreshing escape from Stralsund.
imageAlt: Chalk cliffs of Königsstuhl in Jasmund National Park, Rügen Island

name: Stralsund
blurb: Stralsund, a UNESCO-listed gem on the Baltic Sea, dazzles with its Brick Gothic architecture and maritime history. Expect fresh fish, sea breezes, and stunning views from ancient church towers.
imageAlt: View of Stralsund's Alter Markt (Old Market Square) with the historic Brick Gothic Town Hall and St. Nicholas' Church, bathed in golden afternoon light, with people strolling below.

name: Black Forest Scenic Drive
blurb: Germany's oldest and most famous high-altitude highway, tracking a pine-forested ridge with sweeping views of the Rhine Valley and glacial lakes.
imageAlt: The winding Schwarzwaldhochstraße (B500) highway cutting through dense pine forests of the Black Forest under a clear sky.

name: Usedom Island
blurb: Trade the university town for Germany's sunniest Baltic escape, where grand 19th-century pier architecture meets wild sandy beaches and fresh fish rolls.
imageAlt: The historic wooden pier of Ahlbeck stretching into the Baltic Sea at sunrise

name: Strasbourg
blurb: A mesmerizing blend of French elegance and German grit, where medieval timber-framed houses line canalways under the shadow of a single-spire gothic giant.
imageAlt: Strasbourg - Half-timbered medieval houses along the Ill River in the historic Petite France district

name: Bonn
blurb: The former West German capital is far more than a political relic; it is a relaxed, museum-stuffed riverside city where Beethoven's legacy meets Germany's best cherry blossoms.
imageAlt: Bonn - The bright pink cherry blossoms framing the historic houses of Heerstraße in the Altstadt.

name: Heidelberg
blurb: A direct, practical guide to Heidelberg: how to skip the castle lines, where to find authentic student taverns, and the best panoramic views.
imageAlt: Heidelberg Castle and the Old Bridge overlooking the Neckar River at sunset.

name: Aachen
blurb: Charlemagne's imperial capital is now a lively student hub of thermal springs, cobblestone alleys, and legendary gingerbread, sitting right where Germany, Belgium, and the Netherlands collide.
imageAlt: The Gothic facade of Aachen City Hall overlooking the historic Katschhof square at dusk.

name: Berchtesgaden & Eagle's Nest
blurb: Scale the Bavarian Alps to explore Adolf Hitler's former mountain retreat, stand among jagged alpine peaks, and uncover the dark history of the Obersalzberg complex.
imageAlt: The panoramic alpine mountain landscape looking out from the Kehlsteinhaus summit.

name: Berlin
blurb: A gritty, unvarnished look at Germany's capital from Cold War scars to world-class techno and cheap street eats.
imageAlt: The iconic Brandenburg Gate illuminated at dusk in central Berlin

name: Dachau Memorial Site
blurb: A deeply moving, heavy, yet essential look at Germany's first concentration camp, located just outside Munich.
imageAlt: The infamous Jourhaus entrance gate at Dachau with the inscription Arbeit macht frei.

name: Hallstatt
blurb: Discover the fairytale alpine village of Hallstatt. Learn how to beat the daytime crowds, find the best lake views, and explore the world's oldest salt mine.
imageAlt: The iconic postcard view of Hallstatt showing the Evangelical church steeple against the calm waters of Hallstätter See and dramatic alpine peaks.

name: Munich
blurb: Bavaria's grand capital pairs stately neoclassical architecture and world-class museums with the unhurried, beer-garden-drenched ease of Gemütlichkeit.
imageAlt: The scenic view of the New Town Hall (Neues Rathaus) at Marienplatz in Munich city center.

name: Neuschwanstein
blurb: Escape the city to see King Ludwig II's fairytale masterpiece nestled in the Bavarian Alps.
imageAlt: The white towers of Neuschwanstein Castle rising above dense green pine forests against an alpine backdrop.

name: Potsdam
blurb: A grand playground of Prussian kings, spectacular royal estates, and vast formal gardens located just a short train ride from the capital.
imageAlt: The beautiful terraced vineyards leading up to Sanssouci Palace in Potsdam

name: Sachsenhausen Memorial
blurb: A haunting, deeply educational encounter with World War II and Cold War history at one of the earliest Nazi concentration camps.
imageAlt: The iron gates of Sachsenhausen Memorial featuring the infamous inscription Arbeit Macht Frei

name: Salzburg
blurb: A Baroque masterpiece framed by alpine peaks, Salzburg effortlessly blends classical musical heritage with a vibrant, historic beer culture.
imageAlt: Panoromic view of Salzburg historic old town with the fortress sitting on the hilltop

name: Untersberg Mountain
blurb: Escape the city for dramatic alpine views, high-altitude hiking trails, and a legendary mountain peak straddling the Austrian-German border.
imageAlt: The dramatic rocky ridge of Untersberg Mountain looking out over the surrounding alpine valley

name: Bad Ischl
blurb: Trade the Alpine crowds of Hallstatt for the grand imperial spa town of Bad Ischl, famous for its thermal waters, royal palace, and world-class pastries.
imageAlt: The yellow facade of the historic imperial Kaiservilla surrounded by manicured green gardens in Bad Ischl.

name: Bad Zwischenahn
blurb: Escape the city for a peaceful day at the Ammerland region's premier lake resort, packed with lush gardens, smoked delicacies, and classic thatched architecture.
imageAlt: The historic windmill sitting on the edge of the tranquil Zwischenahner Meer lake.

name: Bremen
blurb: Forget Berlin's grit or Munich's polish. Bremen pairs medieval fairy-tale quarters with a rowdy, maritime grit and an obsession with local beer.
imageAlt: The historic Marktplatz in Bremen featuring the Gothic Town Hall and the famous Roland Statue under clear skies.

name: Bremerhaven
blurb: Germany's rugged North Sea gateway ditches fairytale aesthetics for industrial weight, world-class modern museums, and some of the best raw seafood rolls you will ever eat.
imageAlt: Bremerhaven skyline showing the modern glass architecture of the Havenwelten district against the Weser River dike.

name: Cuxhaven
blurb: Escape to where the Elbe meets the North Sea for windy beaches, historical lighthouses, and a unique walk across the ocean floor.
imageAlt: The Kugelbake wooden beacon standing on the shoreline of Cuxhaven under a dramatic North Sea sky

name: Dachstein Krippenstein
blurb: Escape the crowded lakeside streets of Hallstatt for the jaw-dropping dramatic heights of the Dachstein Alps, complete with subterranean ice worlds and a thrilling walk over a 400-meter drop.
imageAlt: The Five Fingers viewing platform hanging over the sheer cliff face of Dachstein Krippenstein

name: Gosausee Alpine Lakes
blurb: A dramatic trio of glacial lakes framed by the jagged peaks of the Dachstein Alps, offering the perfect outdoor escape just outside Hallstatt.
imageAlt: The crystal clear waters of Vorderer Gosausee reflecting the snow-dusted Dachstein Glacier under a blue sky

name: Hamburg
blurb: Germany's fierce, water-obsessed northern hub where grit meets high culture along legendary canals and harbor docks.
imageAlt: The historic illuminated Speicherstadt warehouse district reflecting in the canal waters at dusk

name: Ludwigslust Palace
blurb: Swap Schwerin for the 'Versailles of the North' to wander through opulent paper-mache ballrooms and a sprawling 127-hectare forest park.
imageAlt: The grand, symmetrical neoclassical facade of Ludwigslust Palace looking over its wide stone basin.

name: Lübeck
blurb: A striking medieval island city wrapped by the Trave River, famous as the historic capital of the Hanseatic League.
imageAlt: The iconic twin-towered medieval Holstentor gate in Lübeck under a clear sky

name: Lüneburg
blurb: A gorgeous medieval salt-mining town of brick Gothic facades, crooked half-timbered lanes, and water-side beer gardens that escaped WWII entirely unscathed.
imageAlt: The historic Alter Kran crane standing over the Ilmenau river at the Stintmarkt waterfront in Lüneburg

name: Oldenburg
blurb: A handsomely understated ducal city submerged in greenery, where bikes outnumber cars and North German hospitality rules the historic cobblestones.
imageAlt: A grand yellow building with historic Neoclassical and Renaissance architecture under a soft sky

name: Poel Island
blurb: Escape the city for wide sandy beaches, a historic 13th-century church, fresh fish buns, and the tranquil pace of Baltic island life.
imageAlt: Panoramic view of Kirchdorf harbour on Poel Island with small boats moored along the pier

name: Schwerin
blurb: A stunning lakeside capital dominated by a fairytale island palace, offering rich history and a beautifully quiet northern German escape.
imageAlt: The golden spires of Schwerin Palace reflecting across the calm waters of the lake at sunset.

name: Travemünde
blurb: Trade the brick Gothic spires of Lübeck for the breezy Baltic coast, historic sailing ships, and the ultimate seaside street food.
imageAlt: Traditional wicker beach chairs lined up along the sandy Travemünde shore.

name: Wismar
blurb: A UNESCO-listed Hanseatic gem on Germany's Baltic coast famed for monumental Brick Gothic churches, a historic old harbor, and fresh fish buns straight off the boats.
imageAlt: The colorful historic facades and the iconic Renaissance Wasserkunst pavilion in the central Market Square of Wismar.

name: Worpswede
blurb: Escape into the peaceful moorlands and explore the historic red-brick expressionist architecture and studios of Germany's most famous artists' colony.
imageAlt: The red-brick expressionist facade of the Grosse Kunstschau museum in Worpswede surrounded by green trees.
